propositions: Publication is triggered by pushing a v* tag with no gate verifying the tagged SHA is an ancestor of reviewed main, allowing an arbitrary commit to publish.
The three version values in pyproject.toml, server.json top-level, and server.json packages[].version are kept in lockstep only by a manual runbook, risking disagreeing published versions.
The release should be re-anchored to a workflow_dispatch-from-main trigger that builds and publishes directly via PyPA Trusted Publishing (OIDC), eliminating the tag-relay and raw-tag publish path.
A pre-build job must assert exact version equality across the dispatch input and all manifests plus assert main-HEAD ancestry before build.
A release-time preflight must query the GitHub environment API and fail closed if the pypi environment lacks required reviewers or a main-only deployment-branch policy.
Guard helpers (version-lockstep, ancestry, env-preflight parsing) must be unit-tested and demonstrated to fire in a real dispatch run without any live publish side effect.
